preflight_quotes.audit_doc: Option A — Smallest: data-integrity smoke. A single Playwright test that boots /play, waits for window.__phaserGame to exist, then evaluates ... runRuntimeAudit ... and asserts passed === true with errors.length === 0.
preflight_quotes.package_json: "playwright": "^1.59.1" — present in devDependencies; no existing test:* npm scripts.
preflight_quotes.runtimeAudit_js: export async function runRuntimeAudit({ silent = false } = {}) { ... return { errors: allErrors, warnings: allWarnings, passed }; } — module returned the audit result but did NOT expose it on window prior to this dispatch.
preflight_quotes.progressionReachabilityAudit_js: progressionReachabilityAudit.js — DEV-only soft-lock detector. Catches the bridge_collapse bug class: a quest is started but the resources/scenes it needs are gated behind that very quest.
preflight_quotes.GameContainer_jsx: if (import.meta.env.DEV) { window.__phaserGame = game; runRuntimeAudit(); } — at GameContainer.jsx:297-300 (file is at src/renderer/game/GameContainer.jsx, not src/renderer/GameContainer.jsx as referenced in the dispatch).
preflight_quotes.scripts_check_mjs: scripts/check-app.mjs uses chromium.launch directly (plain `playwright`, not `playwright/test`) and exits 1 on failure — the existing ad-hoc smoke pattern the audit references.
preflight_quotes.swarm_orchestrator_md: Use `git add <specific files>` ONLY — the orchestrator guardrails warn against blanket adds during parallel dispatches; this dispatch staged only its own files.

notes.runtime_audit_modified: true — added 5 LOC at end of runRuntimeAudit() in src/renderer/game/systems/runtimeAudit.js to assign window.__runtimeAuditResult = result when typeof window !== 'undefined' && import.meta.env?.DEV. Diff: replaced single `return { ... }` line with named-result assignment + DEV-guarded window assignment + return.
notes.playwright_test_list_output:
Listing tests:
  [chromium] > runtime-audit.smoke.spec.js:16:7 > runtime audit smoke > runtime audit reports zero errors at boot
Total: 1 test in 1 file
notes.webserver_command_choice: Used 'npm run dev:react' (Vite-only) rather than 'npm run dev' (Electron+Vite) per the explicit dispatch decision: 'The smoke test runs against the Vite dev server in chromium, not Electron.' npm run dev would unnecessarily spawn Electron alongside Vite; dev:react is the minimal Vite-only command that matches the design intent.
notes.playwright_runner: Used 'playwright/test' (re-exported from the plain `playwright` package as of 1.38+); confirmed via node -e "require('playwright/test')" — no new dependencies required.
notes.parallel_agent_safety: Did NOT stage .claude/swarm/state.json (parallel orchestrator state) or src/renderer/game/data/discoveryUnlocks.js (other agent's work). Used git add with explicit file list, NOT git add -A.
known_caveats: Test was NOT executed per dispatch rule (npx playwright test --list ONLY). Runtime pass/fail unverified — listed as next-action for the user.
Chromium browser binary may not be installed; user must run `npm run test:e2e:install` once before first run.
The smoke test depends on import.meta.env.DEV-only handles; it cannot run against a production build.
Phaser 3 in headless chromium can occasionally hit WebGL context init issues; if the test fails on `waitForGameBoot`, check the Vite server logs and the trace artifact at playwright-report/.

playwright.config.js webServer auto-starts `npm run dev:react`. If port 5173 is occupied by a prior dev session that did not fully clean up, reuseExistingServer:true will reuse it; otherwise the test fails fast with a port conflict.
